title: fractalart
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: fractalart
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install fractalart in Development mode
# make sure fractalart package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to fractalart
$ nbdev_prepare

title: fractalart
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/eandreas/fractalart.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c eandreas fractalart
or from pypi
$ pip install fractalart


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.

title: fractalart
text:
How to use
Fill me in please! Don’t forget code examples:

1+1

2



m = Mandelbrot()
m.render()
m.equalize_histogram()
m.plot()

title: fractal

Fractal

 Fractal (width:int=1200, height:int=1200)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.

source


Mandelbrot

 Mandelbrot (x_min:float=-2.5, x_max:float=1.5, y_min:float=-2.0,
             y_max:float=2.0, width:int=600, height:int=600,
             max_iter:int=200, order:int=2)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.

m = Mandelbrot(order=2)
m.resolution = 600, 600
m.max_iter = 200
a = m.render()
m.equalize_histogram()
m.plot()


source


MandelbrotCrossTrap

 MandelbrotCrossTrap (x_min:float=-2.5, x_max:float=1.5, y_min:float=-2.0,
                      y_max:float=2.0, width:int=600, height:int=600,
                      max_iter:int=200, order:int=2)

Abstract base class for generating fractal images.

m = MandelbrotCrossTrap(order=2)
m.resolution = 600, 600
m.max_iter = 200
a = m.render()
m.equalize_histogram()
m.plot()

title: image
text:
source

Image

 Image (width:int=1200, height:int=1200)

Abstract base class for image rendering and manipulation.
